summary: Completed frontend-only validation of caption accessibility improvements on public preview and reusable package source. Requested flows for transcript click-to-seek, search/filter metadata, theme/size/spacing/panel controls, keyboard shortcuts, and sign overlay modes passed.

issues: Transcript auto-scroll uses smooth scroll unconditionally; reduced-motion preference is not respected in JS behavior.

action_items: Respect prefers-reduced-motion in transcript auto-scroll logic by switching scrollIntoView behavior to 'auto' when reduced motion is enabled.
critical_code_review_comments: Reusable package source includes requested accessibility controls and sign overlay modes with matching behavior to live preview.
No blocking frontend functional defects were observed in requested flows.
Minor accessibility gap: smooth transcript auto-scroll should follow reduced-motion user preference at runtime.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public preview URL tested: https://caption-player-demo.preview.emergentagent.com. Verified controls and keyboard shortcuts with Playwright; transcript click changed video currentTime to expected cue start and overlay mode toggles worked for off/PiP/split.
rca of the issue: RCA (minor): CSS has reduced-motion media query, but transcript navigation still calls scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' }) in component logic, so reduced-motion users may still experience animation. Mitigation: detect prefers-reduced-motion in JS and use behavior: 'auto'.
